subject: Project Management
title: The Great Pyramid of Giza
description: A quick, practical guide to The Great Pyramid of Giza.

hook.text: The Great Pyramid took 20 years, 20,000 workers, and zero computers.

buildup.text: Teams of 20 quarried and hauled 2.3 million limestone blocks into precise position.

discovery.text: Workers were fed, housed, and medically treated — a logistical feat as grand as the structure.

twist.text: Archaeology found no evidence of slave labor — these were skilled, organized professionals.

climax.text: 4,500 years later, the pyramid still stands. The first mega-project was a masterclass.

punchline.text: History's greatest build had no project plan.

quiz.question: What did recent archaeology reveal about the pyramid's workforce?
options: They were skilled professionals, not slaves
They were all prisoners of war
They worked only during harvest season
